mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) signals robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and equity prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient of RSAFS with 20-day forward returns remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative mean returns in expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates a tightening labor market that begins to generate wage pressure and inflation expectations, prompting the Federal Reserve to adopt a more hawkish stance, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or the negative signal fails to be statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year sliding window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent above-trend growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) creates an overheating environment that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy preemptively, reducing equity valuations through higher discount rates and earnings pressure.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions are met, forward 20-day returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the joint signal has an information coefficient below -0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held near zero, monetary policy is accommodative, reducing the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and encouraging risk-taking in equities, which produces positive forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: zero interest rate policy (ZIRP) / post-financial crisis low-rate regime
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.50%, forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the positive return asymmetry is not present (mean return not significantly greater than zero at 5% level over any 5-year window).

mechanism_narrative: A steady increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing and resource utilization, which signals a healthy economy with rising corporate profits, supporting higher equity prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The rank IC of INDPRO with 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window, or the positive signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.2 in trending markets.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 1.5 million units signal strong residential construction activity, which has broad positive multiplier effects on employment, consumer spending, and financial sector confidence, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1500, forward 20-day returns are not positive in more than 55% of observations, or the mean return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low (below 4%) and the federal funds rate is elevated (above 2%), the economy is in a mature stage of the cycle where the output gap closes and the risk of a policy-induced slowdown increases, which historically leads to negative equity returns over the short term.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions are met, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the joint signal's information coefficient is above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a contraction in aggregate economic output, which is a strong predictor of recession and further declines in corporate earnings, leading to sustained negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Following a negative GDP print (< -0.5% QoQ), forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the signal has a positive mean return (p > 0.10 in one-sided test).
